edges.description: Loss of pyruvate carboxylase activity compromises glucose production.
Loss of pyruvate carboxylase activity compromises oxaloacetate-dependent metabolic flux.
Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency causes pyruvate/lactate accumulation.
intermediate_mechanisms: Reduced pyruvate-to-oxaloacetate conversion leaves pyruvate available for lactate production.

edges.description: Impaired gluconeogenesis contributes to hypoglycemia in metabolically unstable presentations.
intermediate_mechanisms: Reduced endogenous glucose production during fasting or illness.

edges.description: TCA-cycle flux failure reduces mitochondrial energy production.
Anaplerotic failure contributes to CNS metabolic dysfunction.
intermediate_mechanisms: Citric-acid-cycle substrate limitation and neurometabolic energy imbalance.

edges.description: Mitochondrial metabolic imbalance is associated with secondary nitrogen-disposal abnormalities.
Chronic energy deficit contributes to poor growth and nutritional instability.
intermediate_mechanisms: Systemic energy deficit and recurrent metabolic decompensation.

edges.description: Energy failure in the CNS contributes to encephalopathy, hypotonia, and seizures.
intermediate_mechanisms: Brain energy substrate deficiency and neurometabolic decompensation.

edges.description: Metabolic decompensation in PC deficiency is accompanied by ketonuria.
Lactate accumulation is represented clinically as lactic acidosis.
Lactate accumulation is represented biochemically as increased blood lactate.
Neonatal metabolic acidosis from lactate accumulation can present with tachypnea or respiratory distress.
intermediate_mechanisms: Metabolic acidosis with compensatory tachypnea in early neonatal disease.

edges.description: Urea-cycle perturbation in severe neonatal PC deficiency is associated with elevated ammonia.
Secondary urea-cycle perturbation in severe PC deficiency includes elevated citrulline.
CNS metabolic dysfunction delays neurodevelopment.
Neurometabolic encephalopathy frequently presents with hypotonia.
Severe neonatal neurologic dysfunction can manifest with tremor, hypokinesia, and abnormal ocular movements.
Severe infantile type A disease is part of the life-limiting encephalopathic spectrum of pyruvate carboxylase deficiency.
Severe neonatal type B disease is part of the life-limiting encephalopathic spectrum of pyruvate carboxylase deficiency.
Severe neonatal type B neurologic dysfunction includes cystic periventricular leukomalacia on brain MRI.
Severe neurologic dysfunction can manifest with seizures.
CNS metabolic dysfunction in type C disease can include delayed cerebral myelination.
Increased lactate reports the lactate-accumulation branch created by impaired pyruvate-to-oxaloacetate flux.
The presence of ketonuria is a biochemical readout of metabolic decompensation accompanying the mitochondrial energy deficit in pyruvate carboxylase deficiency.
Elevated citrulline reports the secondary nitrogen-disposal and urea-cycle perturbation seen in severe neonatal pyruvate carboxylase deficiency.

nodes.description: High-amplitude tremor, hypokinesia, and bizarre ocular behavior are the most common neurologic findings reported in severe neonatal type B disease.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency: metabolic characteristics and new neurological aspects.
evidence.snippet: Abnormal movements (high-amplitude tremor and hypokinesia) and bizarre ocular behavior were the most common findings, whereas epilepsy was infrequent.

nodes.description: Delayed cerebral myelination has been reported in attenuated type C pyruvate carboxylase deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency type C; variable presentation and beneficial effect of triheptanoin
evidence.snippet: MRI‐brain showed delayed cerebral myelination.

nodes.description: Neurodevelopmental delay ranges from speech delay in type C to severe encephalopathy in types A and B.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency type C; variable presentation and beneficial effect of triheptanoin
evidence.snippet: Speech development was delayed. MRI-brain showed delayed cerebral myelination.

nodes.description: Poor growth and nutritional instability are common manifestations of the chronic metabolic deficit.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical and molecular characterization of 12 patients with pyruvate carboxylase deficiency treated with triheptanoin.
evidence.snippet: The main biochemical and clinical findings in PC deficiency (PCD) include lactic acidosis, ketonuria, failure to thrive, and neurological dysfunction.

nodes.description: Hyperammonemia may occur in severe pyruvate carboxylase deficiency through secondary urea-cycle perturbation.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency: metabolic characteristics and new neurological aspects.
evidence.snippet: Hyperammoniemia, hypernatremia, and high proline and lysine were frequently detected.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency: metabolic characteristics and new neurological aspects.
evidence.snippet: Hypoglycemia, lactic acidosis, and hypercitrullinemia were invariably found.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated citrulline reports the secondary nitrogen-disposal and urea-cycle perturbation seen in severe neonatal pyruvate carboxylase deficiency.

nodes.description: Hypoglycemia occurs in some patients, especially in the attenuated but still metabolically unstable type C presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency type C; variable presentation and beneficial effect of triheptanoin
evidence.snippet: Patient 2 (P2) presented with episodic ketoacidosis, hyperlactataemia and hypoglycaemia at 2 years of age, with gross motor delay and mild global volume loss on MRI brain.

nodes.description: Hypotonia is a frequent early neurologic sign, especially in the more severe infantile and neonatal presentations.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency type C; variable presentation and beneficial effect of triheptanoin
evidence.snippet: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency (PCD) mostly presents with life-limiting encephalopathy (types A/B).

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency: metabolic characteristics and new neurological aspects.
evidence.snippet: All patients had axial hypotonia and tachypnea during the first hours of life.

nodes.description: Pyruvate carboxylase replenishes oxaloacetate and other TCA-cycle intermediates, so deficiency impairs anaplerotic mitochondrial flux.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical and molecular characterization of 12 patients with pyruvate carboxylase deficiency treated with triheptanoin.
evidence.snippet: The PC homotetramer has a critical role in gluconeogenesis, anaplerosis, neurotransmitter synthesis, and lipogenesis.

nodes.description: Pyruvate carboxylase normally supports gluconeogenesis, so deficiency impairs glucose production during metabolic stress.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical and molecular characterization of 12 patients with pyruvate carboxylase deficiency treated with triheptanoin.
evidence.snippet: The PC homotetramer has a critical role in gluconeogenesis, anaplerosis, neurotransmitter synthesis, and lipogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical and molecular characterization of 12 patients with pyruvate carboxylase deficiency treated with triheptanoin.
evidence.snippet: The main biochemical and clinical findings in PC deficiency (PCD) include lactic acidosis, ketonuria, failure to thrive, and neurological dysfunction.

readouts.interpretation: The presence of ketonuria is a biochemical readout of metabolic decompensation accompanying the mitochondrial energy deficit in pyruvate carboxylase deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical and molecular characterization of 12 patients with pyruvate carboxylase deficiency treated with triheptanoin.
evidence.snippet: The main biochemical and clinical findings in PC deficiency (PCD) include lactic acidosis, ketonuria, failure to thrive, and neurological dysfunction.

readouts.interpretation: Increased lactate reports the lactate-accumulation branch created by impaired pyruvate-to-oxaloacetate flux.

nodes.description: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency causes recurrent or persistent lactate accumulation as a dominant biochemical disease feature.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical and molecular characterization of 12 patients with pyruvate carboxylase deficiency treated with triheptanoin.
evidence.snippet: The main biochemical and clinical findings in PC deficiency (PCD) include lactic acidosis, ketonuria, failure to thrive, and neurological dysfunction.

nodes.description: Recurrent or persistent lactic acidosis is the dominant biochemical abnormality across pyruvate carboxylase deficiency phenotypes.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical and molecular characterization of 12 patients with pyruvate carboxylase deficiency treated with triheptanoin.
evidence.snippet: The main biochemical and clinical findings in PC deficiency (PCD) include lactic acidosis, ketonuria, failure to thrive, and neurological dysfunction.

nodes.description: Failure of pyruvate carboxylase-dependent metabolism produces systemic energy deficit that contributes to growth failure and neurologic dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical and molecular characterization of 12 patients with pyruvate carboxylase deficiency treated with triheptanoin.
evidence.snippet: The main biochemical and clinical findings in PC deficiency (PCD) include lactic acidosis, ketonuria, failure to thrive, and neurological dysfunction.

nodes.description: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency disrupts brain energy metabolism and myelination, producing developmental and structural neurologic abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency type C; variable presentation and beneficial effect of triheptanoin
evidence.snippet: Speech development was delayed. MRI-brain showed delayed cerebral myelination.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular profiles of three Sri Lankan neonates with pyruvate carboxylase deficiency
evidence.snippet: The other proband with normal citrulline, lysine, moderate lactate, paraventricular cystic lesions, bony deformities, and a novel missense, homozygous variant c.2746G>C [p.(Asp916His)] in the PC gene, biochemically favoured type A.

nodes.description: Cystic periventricular leukomalacia is the predominant brain MRI finding in severe neonatal type B pyruvate carboxylase deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency: metabolic characteristics and new neurological aspects.
evidence.snippet: Brain magnetic resonance imaging mostly disclosed cystic periventricular leukomalacia.

nodes.description: Biallelic PC dysfunction impairs the mitochondrial pyruvate carboxylase complex, reducing the conversion of pyruvate to oxaloacetate.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical and molecular characterization of 12 patients with pyruvate carboxylase deficiency treated with triheptanoin.
evidence.snippet: Pyruvate carboxylase (PC) deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive mitochondrial neurometabolic disorder of energy deficit resulting in high morbidity and mortality, with limited therapeutic options.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency: mechanisms, mimics and anaplerosis.
evidence.snippet: Pyruvate carboxylase (PC) is a regulated mitochondrial enzyme that catalyzes the conversion of pyruvate to oxaloacetate, a critical transition that replenishes citric acid cycle intermediates and facilitates other biosynthetic reactions that drive anabolism.

nodes.description: Severe neonatal pyruvate carboxylase deficiency may present with respiratory distress in the first hours of life.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular profiles of three Sri Lankan neonates with pyruvate carboxylase deficiency
evidence.snippet: All three developed respiratory distress within the first few hours of birth.

nodes.description: Seizures occur in severe pyruvate carboxylase deficiency as part of the neurometabolic encephalopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency: metabolic characteristics and new neurological aspects.
evidence.snippet: Abnormal movements (high-amplitude tremor and hypokinesia) and bizarre ocular behavior were the most common findings, whereas epilepsy was infrequent.

nodes.description: Triheptanoin is used as an anaplerotic odd-chain triglyceride therapy, with mixed overall results but apparent benefit in some type C patients.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical and molecular characterization of 12 patients with pyruvate carboxylase deficiency treated with triheptanoin.
evidence.snippet: Use of the anaplerotic agent triheptanoin on a limited number of individuals with PCD has had mixed results.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency type C; variable presentation and beneficial effect of triheptanoin
evidence.snippet: Subsequently, hospitalisations during intercurrent illnesses decreased, post-exertional hyperlactataemia resolved and exercise tolerance improved.

nodes.description: Life-limiting encephalopathy is a dominant clinical feature of the severe infantile type A form of pyruvate carboxylase deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency type C; variable presentation and beneficial effect of triheptanoin
evidence.snippet: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency (PCD) mostly presents with life-limiting encephalopathy (types A/B).

nodes.description: Life-limiting encephalopathy is a dominant clinical feature of the severe neonatal type B form of pyruvate carboxylase deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency type C; variable presentation and beneficial effect of triheptanoin
evidence.snippet: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency (PCD) mostly presents with life-limiting encephalopathy (types A/B).

nodes.description: Severe pyruvate carboxylase deficiency can perturb mitochondrial nitrogen handling, contributing to hyperammonemia in some presentations.

evidence.reference_title: Pyruvate carboxylase deficiency: metabolic characteristics and new neurological aspects.
evidence.snippet: Hypoglycemia, lactic acidosis, and hypercitrullinemia were invariably found. Hyperammoniemia, hypernatremia, and high proline and lysine were frequently detected.
